root_text.ud5.4:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud5.4:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā kumārakā antarā ca sāvatthiṁ antarā ca jetavanaṁ macchake bādhenti.
root_text.ud5.4:2.1: Atha kho bhagavā pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya sāvatthiṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.
root_text.ud5.4:2.2: Addasā kho bhagavā te sambahule kumārake antarā ca sāvatthiṁ antarā ca jetavanaṁ macchake bādhente.
root_text.ud5.4:2.3: Disvāna yena te kumārakā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā te kumārake etadavoca:
root_text.ud5.4:2.4: “bhāyatha vo, tumhe kumārakā, dukkhassa, appiyaṁ vo dukkhan”ti?
root_text.ud5.4:2.5: “Evaṁ, bhante, bhāyāma mayaṁ, bhante, dukkhassa, appiyaṁ no dukkhan”ti.
root_text.ud5.4:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud5.4:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud5.4:1.3: Now at that time, between Sāvatthī and the Jeta Grove, several boys were tormenting some fish.
translation_text.ud5.4:2.1: Then the Buddha robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, entered Sāvatthī for alms.
translation_text.ud5.4:2.2: He saw the boys tormenting the fish.
translation_text.ud5.4:2.3: He went to them and said,
translation_text.ud5.4:2.4: “Boys, do you fear pain? Do you dislike pain?”
translation_text.ud5.4:3.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud5.4:5.1: If you should do a bad deed,
translation_text.ud5.4:5.3: you won’t be freed from suffering,
translation_text.ud5.4:5.4: though you fly away and flee.”

variant_text.ud5.4:5.4: upeccapi → upeccāpi (sya-all, pts-vp-pli1 pv19:18 [Dhanapālaseṭṭhipetavatthu]); upaccapi (mr); uppaccapi (?)

reference_text.ud5.4:1.1: bj24.228, cck25.123, mc0.163, ms18Ud_377, msdiv44, pts-vp-pli51
